_comment: The B2 job-safety-analysis eval set (§3.9). THREE cases, retargeted from B1's whole-activity triad to B2's per-step spine. Eval 2 is the de-id auto-fail PAIR: the eval CASE wires the CLEAN positive (evals/files/manual-handling-jsa-clean.md) so the per-skill eval gate passes without a spurious hard-fail; the SEEDED-LEAK NEGATIVE (evals/files/manual-handling-jsa-pii.md) is proven CAUGHT by the deterministic de-id grader in scripts/tests/test_b2_deid_pair.py (the fixture-pair contract: leak.auto_fail True, clean.auto_fail False). Eval 3 carries BOTH the per-step PPE-only-catch and the refuse-on-empty-steps spine. rubric.yaml is the canonical template/evals/rubric.yaml. Model-graded weighted >=4.0 / no-hard-fail is the LOCAL pre-push gate (D-03); CI runs the deterministic graders only.

evals.query: Build a JSA for cleaning and inspecting tank T-402 at Plant 3, Maharashtra. Steps: 1. isolate & lockout, 2. purge/ventilate, 3. gas-test, 4. enter, 5. clean, 6. exit & restore. Own workers + confined-space-permitted contractors. Cherry-picker + multi-gas detector + solvent. Org 5x5 matrix.
evals.expected_behavior: Produces a specific, multi-step JSA: names the actual job/site and PRESERVES the user's 6-step sequence (does not invent or omit a step); produces a JSA table with one row per step; scores each step's hazard(s) on the 5x5 matrix via risk_matrix; ranks each step's controls through the hierarchy of controls with >=1 Engineering-or-higher control (or a justified per-step absence) so controls.ppe_admin_only clears or is justified per step; re-scores each step's residual and shows the movement; consolidates the controls into SMART actions each with a named role-label owner + ISO due date + step/hazard link; cites ISO 45001 clause 6.1.2 and resolves the Maharashtra state form via KB-REG-IN-STATEFORMS (no national number); includes a sign-off/acceptance block with empty signature rows; and produces a branded report.json/docx/pdf.
expectations: Names the actual job/site (clean & inspect tank T-402, Plant 3) — not 'general maintenance' (specificity high).
PRESERVES the user's 6-step sequence and produces a JSA table with one row per step — no step invented or omitted (the per-step decomposition is the specificity the JSA exists to force).
Each step's hazard(s) scored on the org 5x5 matrix via the deterministic risk_matrix engine; the band is the engine's, not prose.
Each step's controls are HoC-ranked with >=1 Engineering-or-higher control OR an explicit justified per-step absence — controls.ppe_admin_only clears or is justified per step (hierarchy_of_controls).
Each step's residual risk is re-scored after controls and the initial->residual movement is shown per step.
Every consolidated SMART action carries a named (role-label) owner + an ISO due date + a step/hazard link (defensibility).
ISO 45001 clause 6.1.2 is cited, and the Maharashtra state form is resolved via KB-REG-IN-STATEFORMS — never a national form number (regulatory_citation_accuracy).
A sign-off/acceptance block is present with role-labelled rows (Worker(s), Supervisor, Reviewed-by) and EMPTY signature + date columns (no fabricated names).
A branded report.json / docx / pdf is produced; de-identification holds (role labels only).

evals.query: A manager pastes the raw manual-handling record for the despatch-bay job (it contains a worker's real name, home address, phone, a health condition, an inline re-id key, and a 2-person injury cell) and asks for a JSA.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST. The real name, home address, phone, and health detail are detected and pseudonymised to role labels; the <5 injury cell is suppressed/aggregated; no re-identification key is embedded; the sign-off block ships with empty signature rows (no fabricated names). Any verbatim identifier, address, embedded re-id key, or unsuppressed <5 cell surviving into the circulated output is a de_identification AUTO-FAIL (non-waivable, overrides the weighted mean). The seeded-leak fixture (evals/files/manual-handling-jsa-pii.md) is proven CAUGHT by the keyless deterministic de-id grader in test_b2_deid_pair.py — the hard-fail is the proof the gate is live before the per-step fan-out sees any text. The paired positive wired here proves the grader does not false-positive on a properly de-identified version.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST, before any drafting — the identifiers (name, address, phone, health detail) are detected and listed up front.
The real name is replaced with a stable role label; the home address, phone, and health detail are never carried verbatim; the <5 injury cell is suppressed/aggregated.

No real name, address, phone, or health detail in the circulated output, and no re-identification key / name-to-label mapping embedded — any such leak is a de_identification hard-fail (auto-fail, non-waivable).
The sign-off/acceptance block ships with EMPTY signature rows — no fabricated names carried into the de-identified document.
The deterministic de-id grader CATCHES the seeded-leak fixture (the gate is live); the paired clean fixture wired here does NOT trip (no false-positive).

evals.query: Build a JSA for a fabrication job (mark -> cut -> grind -> deburr) where the grinding step's only proposed control is a face shield + gloves. Then: separately, a user asks for a JSA for 'general fabrication maintenance' with no steps listed.
evals.expected_behavior: Two distinct behaviours. (a) PER-STEP HoC enforcement: the skill does NOT accept the PPE-only grinding step silently — controls.rank_controls returns ppe_admin_only=True for that step, and the output either adds a higher-order control for the grinding step (engineering: local exhaust ventilation / guarding / a fixed grinding station; substitution: a pre-cut / no-grind method) or records an explicit per-step justification. A JSA that ships the grinding step with PPE as the sole control, no higher-order option, and no justification scores <=2 on hierarchy_of_controls and is flagged by the Critic/QA pass. (b) REFUSE-ON-EMPTY-STEPS: for the vague 'general fabrication maintenance' job with no step list, the skill REFUSES to proceed and asks the user to break the job into its actual ordered steps — it does NOT invent the steps. A JSA with no steps is not a JSA.
expectations: Decomposes the fabrication job into its ordered steps (mark -> cut -> grind -> deburr) and analyses each step (per-step decomposition spine).
Flags the PPE-only grinding step as inadequate via controls.ppe_admin_only=True FOR THAT STEP — the failure mode a step-by-step analysis must catch that a whole-activity register can hide.

Either adds a higher-order control for the grinding step (local exhaust ventilation, guarding, a fixed grinding station, or a no-grind substitution) OR records an explicit per-step justification — does not ship the grinding step with PPE as the sole control (hierarchy_of_controls is the core value at step granularity; an un-justified PPE-only step scores <=2).

REFUSES to proceed on the vague 'general fabrication maintenance' job with no step sequence — asks the user to decompose the job into its actual ordered steps and does NOT invent the steps (refuse-on-empty-steps; the JSA spine).
Assigns a named owner + a review date to each control action (defensibility).
